apiKey.title: API key
apiKey.hintMain: Supports multiple keys. Paste comma/newline separated keys in Key settings; keys rotate automatically.
apiKey.rotationHint: Multiple keys are rotated automatically (comma/newline separated).
apiKey.bulkPlaceholder: Paste one or more API keys (one per line or comma-separated)
apiKey.bulkHint: Comma/newline separated. Duplicates are removed automatically.
apiKey.inlineEditBlocked: Multiple API keys are configured. Open “Manage all” to edit them without overwriting the rest.
apiKey.inlineHintMulti: Multiple API keys are configured. The inline field only shows the first key; use “Manage all” to edit the full list.
apiKey.listDescription: Manage multiple API keys and run connectivity checks.
apiKey.empty: No keys. Click Add to create one.
apiKey.errorUrlLike: Enter an API key here, not an API base URL. Put the URL in the API base field.
apiKey.inlineManageMulti: This provider already has multiple keys. Edit them in Manage all; the inline field will not overwrite a multi-key config.

apiBase.title: API base
apiBase.previewTransportDependent: Preview base: {{url}} (the exact chat endpoint depends on the model transport)
apiBase.emptyHint: API base is empty. Please set it before use.
apiBase.overrideHint: Only fill this for a custom proxy or gateway. Leave empty to derive the official endpoint from the dedicated auth fields.
apiBase.overridePlaceholder: Leave empty to use the official default

models.title: Models
models.empty: No models yet. Click the button below to add one.

manageDialog.title: {{provider}} models
manageDialog.description: Manage models, groups, user-facing model types, and system-detected semantics for this provider.
manageDialog.notSupported: This provider does not support fetching a model catalog via /models. Please add model IDs manually and use Health Check to verify connectivity.
manageDialog.importBlockedUnknownProtocol: The runtime protocol for this model is still unresolved. Update the registry or set the protocol manually before importing it.
manageDialog.importBlockedProviderCatalogTypeUnsuppor: The official catalog returned {{typeLabel}}, but the extension does not yet have a stable import/runtime protocol for it. It is shown for reference only and cannot be imported.
manageDialog.importBlockedTogetherDedicatedSpeechEndp: The official catalog returned this Together speech model, but it requires a Together Dedicated Endpoint. The extension does not yet support importing or invoking it by endpoint name, so it is shown for reference only and cannot be imported.
manageDialog.importBlockedTranscriptionRuntimeUnavail: The official catalog returned this transcription model, but the current provider does not yet have a runnable transcription-api runtime in the extension. It is shown for reference only and cannot be imported.
manageDialog.importBlockedSpeechRuntimeUnavailable: The official catalog returned this speech model, but the current provider does not yet have a runnable speech-api runtime in the extension. It is shown for reference only and cannot be imported.
manageDialog.importBlockedModerationRuntimeUnavailabl: The official catalog returned this moderation model, but the current provider does not yet have a runnable moderation-api runtime in the extension. It is shown for reference only and cannot be imported.
manageDialog.importBlockedVideoRuntimeUnavailable: The official catalog returned this video model, but the current provider does not yet have a runnable video-api runtime in the extension. It is shown for reference only and cannot be imported.

modelDialog.description: Configure model ID, name, group, manual model-type overrides, and the system read-only semantic metadata.

errors.duplicateId: Model ID "{{id}}" already exists. Please use a unique model ID.

modelDialog.registryPlaceholder: Enter a model ID to preview how the current Provider resolves it in Model Registry.

sectionDescriptions.basic: Mirrors the desktop layout while keeping only the fields that still participate in the current extension logic.
sectionDescriptions.advanced: The left side only edits the 8 user-facing model types; primary kind, system capabilities, and protocol remain read-only.
sectionDescriptions.registryMetadata: This card always shows system-detected metadata and is never overwritten by the manual types on the left.

modelDialog.modelTypesFollowHint: No manual override is stored yet. The selected state on the left currently comes from system detection.
modelDialog.modelTypesOverrideHint: Manual override is active. Runtime, model lists, and model pickers will all prefer the left-side result.

help.id: The model identifier sent to the API. Must match the provider (e.g. gpt-4o).
help.name: Display name only. Does not affect requests.
help.group: Used for grouping in UI. Does not affect requests.
help.modelTypes: Only the 8 desktop-style user model types are editable here: text generation, image generation, vision, web search, reasoning, tool calling, rerank, and embedding. Image generation now belongs to both the primary-kind layer and the user model type layer. Image generation is exclusive; embedding and rerank remain mutually exclusive; selecting any exclusive type disables the others.
help.userModelTypes: Only the 8 desktop-style user model types are editable here: text generation, image generation, vision, web search, reasoning, tool calling, rerank, and embedding. Image generation now belongs to both the primary-kind layer and the user model type layer. Image generation is exclusive; embedding and rerank remain mutually exclusive; selecting any exclusive type disables the others.
help.systemTransportProtocol: Transport routing is now system-inferred and read-only. If the result is unknown, the current model still lacks enough official catalog signals or stable fallback rules.
help.supportedTextDelta: Whether the model supports streaming text deltas. Disable to fallback to non-streaming for compatibility.
help.registryMetadata: Read-only projection from the unified Model Registry, including base model ID, internal canonicalId, system-detected primary kind, system capabilities, modalities, and resolved protocol.

fields.name: Model name
fields.effectiveUserModelTypesEmpty: The current primary kind does not map to any user model types
fields.systemTransportProtocolUnknown: Not detected yet. The model can still be saved, but runtime requests cannot be routed with an unknown protocol.

headersDialog.title: Custom headers
headersDialog.description: Configure custom HTTP headers (JSON) for this provider.

addProviderDialog.title: Add provider
addProviderDialog.description: Configure name, type, and API base for a custom provider.

fields.name: Provider name
fields.anthropicApiHostHint: Only use this when some models under this provider speak the Anthropic Messages protocol. Leave it empty to reuse the API base.

deleteProvider.desc: Models from this provider will no longer appear in model pickers.

healthDialog.title: Model health check
healthDialog.description: Send probe requests to verify availability and latency.
healthDialog.warning: Health checks send requests. Use with caution. Pay-per-request models may incur additional costs.

loadState.loadingDescription: Editing is temporarily blocked until hydration finishes, so default values cannot overwrite real configuration.
loadState.errorDescription: Editing stays blocked until the saved configuration is loaded successfully, to avoid overwriting existing data.

saveState.unsaved: Current edits have not reached the background runtime yet.

apiOptions.title: API compatibility
apiOptions.isNotSupportImageInputHint: When enabled, requests with image attachments will fail before reaching the provider.
apiOptions.isNotSupportFileInputHint: When enabled, requests with native file attachments will fail before reaching the provider.
apiOptions.isNotSupportStreamOptionsHint: Some OpenAI-compatible gateways don't support include_usage and related fields.
apiOptions.isSupportDeveloperRoleHint: When enabled, system prompts are sent as developer role; when disabled, they stay as system.
apiOptions.isSupportServiceTierHint: Assumed supported by default; disable to stop injecting service tier.
apiOptions.isNotSupportEnableThinkingHint: For Qwen-like models you can control via /think or /no_think suffix.
apiOptions.isNotSupportVerbosityHint: Disable to avoid injecting OpenAI Responses verbosity.

providerOptions.title: Provider-level parameters
providerOptions.hint: Only applied to provider types that support these protocol-level settings.
providerOptions.serviceTierHint: Only injected if supported by the provider.

apiKeyAuth.title: API key auth
apiKeyAuth.hint: Configure the auth header used for provider.apiKey. Custom headers are only extra business headers and do not own authentication.
apiKeyAuth.valuePrefixHint: Leave empty to send the raw API key. Bearer sends “Bearer <key>”.
apiKeyAuth.invalidHeader: Header name is invalid, and Content-Type is not allowed.

rateLimit.title: Rate limit (optional)
rateLimit.hint: Used for throttling/health check scenarios (does not change provider behavior).

notes.title: Notes
notes.placeholder: Stored locally; record rate limits and special params

azure.title: Azure OpenAI
azure.sectionHint: Azure OpenAI supports the legacy deployment endpoint with api-version and the newer /openai/v1 endpoint. This controls the preview and whether runtime sends api-version.
azure.apiShapeHint: Choose deployment + api-version for /openai/deployments/{deployment}. Choose v1 endpoint for /openai/v1.
azure.apiVersionHint: Required for deployment endpoint mode. Runtime sends it as the api-version query parameter.
azure.hint: Switching to v1 endpoint clears API Version and disables api-version. Switching back to deployment mode requires filling API Version again.

newApi.title: NewAPI
newApi.sectionHint: NewAPI can route by model transport. Set a separate Anthropic upstream when Claude-like models use the Anthropic Messages protocol.
newApi.anthropicApiHostHint: Only used for models with transportProtocol=anthropic-messages. Leave empty to reuse the API base.
newApi.hint: This only affects Anthropic Messages routing and does not change the default OpenAI-compatible route.

vertex.title: Vertex AI
vertex.sectionHint: Used for Google Vertex AI (Gemini on Vertex). Service Account requires project and location; express mode API key does not.
vertex.sectionHintAnthropic: Used for Anthropic (Claude) on Vertex AI. Service Account is required with project, location, and service account fields.
vertex.authTypeHint: Service Account uses project, location, and service account fields. API Key uses Vertex express mode.
vertex.projectIdHint: Required in Service Account mode, for example my-gcp-project.
vertex.locationHint: Required in Service Account mode, for example us-central1.
vertex.clientEmailHint: Service Account email, matching client_email in Google credentials.
vertex.privateKeyHint: Service Account PEM private key. Escaped \n line breaks are accepted.
vertex.privateKeyIdHint: Matches private_key_id in Google credentials. Optional.
vertex.apiKeyHint: Vertex express mode API key. projectId/location are not required.
vertex.hint: Vertex AI can switch between Service Account and express mode API key. provider.apiKey is not used.
vertex.hintAnthropic: Vertex Anthropic always uses Service Account and does not support API key mode.

bedrock.title: AWS Bedrock
bedrock.sectionHint: Used for AWS Bedrock. Official Bedrock supports IAM and Bedrock API keys; temporary credentials can include Session Token.
bedrock.authTypeHint: IAM uses Access Key / Secret Key. API Key uses the official AWS Bedrock Bearer token path.
bedrock.regionHint: AWS region used to resolve the Bedrock endpoint, for example us-east-1.
bedrock.sessionTokenHint: Only needed for temporary STS credentials.
bedrock.apiKeyHint: AWS Bedrock API key. The SDK sends it as a Bearer token; provider.apiKey is not used.
bedrock.hint: Bedrock uses IAM or the official Bedrock API key; provider.apiKey is not used.

anthropicCache.title: Anthropic prompt caching
anthropicCache.hint: When token count exceeds the threshold, mark messages with ephemeral cacheControl.
